seo.title: About · Sakyadhita
seo.description: Founded in 1987 in Bodh Gaya, India, Sakyadhita International Association of Buddhist Women connects Buddhist women across traditions, cultures, and continents.

hero.title: Daughters of the Buddha
hero.lede: Founded in 1987 in Bodh Gaya, India — the site of the Buddha's awakening — Sakyadhita has spent nearly four decades connecting Buddhist women across traditions, cultures, and continents.

story.heading: A beacon of inspiration
paragraphs: In 1987, Ayya Khema, Chatsumarn Kabilsingh, and Karma Lekshe Tsomo gathered with a small group of women in Bodh Gaya, India to talk about the difficulties women faced in various Buddhist traditions. The organization was christened Sakyadhita — Daughters of the Buddha.

By establishing and maintaining a communications network among Buddhist women internationally, Sakyadhita has created links among some 400 million Buddhist women worldwide, encouraging and supporting them in practice, scholarship, activism, and the arts.

photos.alt: Participants of the first Sakyadhita conference in 1987

mission.heading: Sakyadhita's seven objectives
mission.sub: A working list, written in 1987 and barely changed since. They keep the organization focused across 45 countries and 19 conferences.

objectives.title: An international alliance
objectives.text: A network of Buddhist women across schools, sanghas, and divisions.

objectives.title: Welfare of women
objectives.text: Advancing the spiritual and secular welfare of the world's women.

objectives.title: Gender equity
objectives.text: In Buddhist education, training, institutions, and ordination.

objectives.title: Harmony among traditions
objectives.text: Dialogue across Buddhist traditions and with other religions.

objectives.title: Research & publications
objectives.text: Scholarship on history, doctrine, practice, and lineage.

objectives.title: Compassionate action
objectives.text: Wisdom carried into the world as social action.

objectives.title: World peace
objectives.text: Promoting peace through the teachings of the Buddha.

cards.imageAlt: Sakyadhita Training and Meditation Centre, Sri Lanka
cards.title: Sakyadhita Training and Meditation Centre
cards.text: Trains Buddhist nuns in Dhamma, Vinaya, and meditation while strengthening the Bhikkhuni Order re-established in Sri Lanka after a thousand-year interruption. Its nuns run counseling, children's Dhamma classes, and disaster-relief work across their communities.

cards.title: Sanghamitra Institute
cards.text: Supported by the Jamyang Foundation, the institute is dedicated to improving life for women and children from economically and socially disadvantaged backgrounds. It offers new learning opportunities in harmony with the region's Buddhist heritage.

branches.heading: A worldwide network
branches.text: Branches and local chapters organize events, run translation projects, and help plan the biennial international conference. Active groups span 45 countries — from Mongolia to Brazil, Strasbourg to Cairo.
